metadata.description: Satya Promise Tracker — key promises made by Indian political leaders, linked to news evidence

promises.promise: Double farmer income by 2022
promises.source_description: Indian Express: Will double farmers' income by 2022, says PM Modi
promises.gemma_reasoning: The target deadline of 2022 has passed and farm income did not double.

evidence_articles.title: Modi government promised to double farm income by 2022 but it has only come down
evidence_articles.rephrased: The report notes that despite the 2022 target date, real farm incomes failed to double, citing agricultural economists and survey assessments.
evidence_articles.content: The DFI Committee recommendations were not fully met by the 2022 deadline, leaving the target of doubled real farmer incomes unfulfilled.

promises.notes: The target year of 2022 has passed. Real agricultural income did not double; NSO survey data indicates average farm income growth remained far below the target.
promises.supporting_quote: The report notes that despite the 2022 target date, real farm incomes failed to double, citing agricultural economists and survey assessments.
promises.importance_reason: high-impact category (farmers/agriculture) + senior role (Prime Minister)

promises.promise: Create 2 crore jobs every year
promises.source_description: BJP Election Manifesto 2014
promises.gemma_reasoning: CMIE data showed rising unemployment post-2016, contradicting the job creation promise.

evidence_articles.title: Unemployment rate at 45-year high, confirms PLFS data
evidence_articles.rephrased: Government's own PLFS data confirmed unemployment reached a 45-year high of 6.1% in 2017-18, contradicting large-scale job creation targets.
evidence_articles.content: The Periodic Labour Force Survey (PLFS) report showed India's unemployment rate stood at a 45-year high, reflecting severe job creation shortfalls.

promises.notes: Unemployment statistics from PLFS and CMIE indicate India did not add 2 crore jobs per year, with youth unemployment rates remaining a major concern.
promises.supporting_quote: Government's own PLFS data confirmed unemployment reached a 45-year high of 6.1% in 2017-18, contradicting large-scale job creation targets.
promises.importance_reason: high-impact category (economy) + senior role (Prime Minister)

promises.promise: Bring back black money from Swiss banks and deposit Rs 15 lakh in every Indian's account
promises.source_description: BJP Election Manifesto 2014 (BJPLibrary) - Black Money section
promises.gemma_reasoning: No black money repatriation happened, and the Rs 15 lakh promise was called a 'jumla'.

evidence_articles.title: FM defends Modi's 'Rs 15L for all' claim
evidence_articles.rephrased: The Finance Minister defended the PM's claim in Parliament, stating the Rs 15 lakh figure was illustrative of the total estimated size of illicit black money stashed abroad.
evidence_articles.content: Finance Minister Arun Jaitley clarified in the Rajya Sabha that the Rs 15 lakh figure was an illustrative amount meant to show the scale of black money stashed in foreign accounts.

promises.notes: The government failed to recover enough black money to deposit Rs 15 lakh in every citizen's account. Party leadership later clarified that the figure was rhetorical rather than a literal policy target.
promises.supporting_quote: The Finance Minister defended the PM's claim in Parliament, stating the Rs 15 lakh figure was illustrative of the total estimated size of illicit black money stashed abroad.
promises.importance_reason: high-impact category (economy) + senior role (Prime Minister)

promises.promise: Build 2 crore pucca houses for the poor by 2022 (PM Awas Yojana)
promises.source_description: PIB: Launch of Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana (PMAY)
promises.gemma_reasoning: Target deadline of 2022 missed, partial completion achieved.

evidence_articles.title: Cabinet approves extension of Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana till December 2024
evidence_articles.rephrased: The Union Cabinet approved the extension of the PMAY-Urban scheme to December 2024, confirming that the original 2022 target of 2 crore houses was not fully completed on time.
evidence_articles.content: The Union Cabinet approved the proposal to extend the implementation period of PMAY-Urban till December 31, 2024, to complete all houses sanctioned till March 31, 2022.

promises.notes: PMAY set a target of 2 crore houses by 2022. While over 1.5 crore houses were built by late 2022, the target was missed and the project deadline was officially extended to March 2024.
promises.supporting_quote: The Union Cabinet approved the extension of the PMAY-Urban scheme to December 2024, confirming that the original 2022 target of 2 crore houses was not fully completed on time.
promises.importance_reason: high-impact category (infrastructure) + senior role (Prime Minister)

promises.promise: Make India a $5 trillion economy by 2024-25
promises.source_description: PIB: Prime Minister sets target of $5 Trillion Economy
promises.gemma_reasoning: Target deadline of 2024 passed, and the $5 trillion GDP goal was not achieved.

evidence_articles.title: India's delayed $5-trillion dream: What IMF's forecasts say
evidence_articles.rephrased: The IMF updated growth forecasts showing that due to the pandemic, India's target of becoming a $5 trillion economy by 2024-25 would be delayed by several years.
evidence_articles.content: IMF projections indicate that the Covid-19 pandemic-induced slowdown has pushed back India's target of reaching a $5-trillion GDP to at least 2027-28.

promises.notes: India missed the target of reaching a $5 trillion GDP by 2024-25, largely due to the economic disruptions of the COVID-19 pandemic. The target has been pushed forward to 2027-28.
promises.supporting_quote: The IMF updated growth forecasts showing that due to the pandemic, India's target of becoming a $5 trillion economy by 2024-25 would be delayed by several years.
promises.importance_reason: high-impact category (economy) + senior role (Prime Minister)

promises.promise: End corruption completely — 'Na khaunga, na khane dunga'
promises.source_description: BJP Election Manifesto 2014 (BJPLibrary) - Governance & Anti-corruption
promises.gemma_reasoning: Multiple corruption scandals occurred during Modi's tenure.

evidence_articles.title: India's rank slips to 85 on Corruption Perception Index
evidence_articles.rephrased: India's ranking on the Corruption Perception Index (CPI) remained stagnant around 85th out of 180 countries, indicating persistent structural corruption challenges.
evidence_articles.content: Transparency International's Corruption Perception Index showed India ranking at 85, with a score of 40, reflecting lack of significant improvement in public sector integrity.

promises.notes: This is a broad vision promise representing PM Modi's governance slogan. Independent corruption indexes (like Transparency International's CPI) show India's score remaining stagnant.
promises.supporting_quote: India's ranking on the Corruption Perception Index (CPI) remained stagnant around 85th out of 180 countries, indicating persistent structural corruption challenges.
promises.importance_reason: high-impact category (corruption/governance) + senior role (Prime Minister)

promises.promise: Electoral bonds will bring transparency to political funding
promises.source_description: PIB: Finance Minister announces details of Electoral Bonds
promises.gemma_reasoning: Electoral bonds were struck down as unconstitutional in Feb 2024.

evidence_articles.title: Supreme Court strikes down Electoral Bonds scheme, calling it unconstitutional
evidence_articles.rephrased: The Supreme Court struck down the anonymous Electoral Bonds scheme, ordering the State Bank of India to stop issuing bonds and disclose all political donor data.
evidence_articles.content: A five-judge Constitution bench headed by Chief Justice D.Y. Chandrachud ruled that the anonymous electoral bonds scheme violates the right to information under Article 19(1)(a).

promises.notes: In February 2024, the Supreme Court of India declared the Electoral Bonds scheme unconstitutional, ruling that anonymous political funding violates the right to information.
promises.supporting_quote: The Supreme Court struck down the anonymous Electoral Bonds scheme, ordering the State Bank of India to stop issuing bonds and disclose all political donor data.
promises.importance_reason: minor: category 'politics' not in high-impact list

promises.promise: Har ghar nal se jal — piped water to every household by 2024
promises.source_description: PIB: PM announces Jal Jeevan Mission (Har Ghar Jal)
promises.gemma_reasoning: Target deadline of 2024 has passed and the promise is likely to be missed.

evidence_articles.title: Centre's potable water mission may miss 2024 target
evidence_articles.rephrased: The report notes that while tap water connections grew from 16% in 2019 to over 65% in 2023, the Jal Jeevan Mission is likely to miss its December 2024 target for 100% coverage.
evidence_articles.content: Official statistics and implementation challenges indicate that the Jal Jeevan Mission may fall short of the 100% piped water connection target by the end of 2024.

promises.notes: The Jal Jeevan Mission achieved over 75% coverage of rural households with tap connections by 2024, missing the 100% target for the year 2024 but making significant progress.
promises.supporting_quote: The report notes that while tap water connections grew from 16% in 2019 to over 65% in 2023, the Jal Jeevan Mission is likely to miss its December 2024 target for 100% coverage.
promises.importance_reason: high-impact category (infrastructure) + senior role (Prime Minister)

promises.promise: Uniform Civil Code implementation
promises.source_description: BJP Sankalp Patra 2019 (BJPLibrary) - Uniform Civil Code section
promises.gemma_reasoning: National UCC not yet implemented.

evidence_articles.title: Uttarakhand Assembly passes Uniform Civil Code Bill
evidence_articles.rephrased: Uttarakhand passed the Uniform Civil Code Bill, making it the first state to implement the code. The law covers marriage, divorce, inheritance, and live-in relationships.
evidence_articles.content: Uttarakhand became the first state in the country to pass the Uniform Civil Code Bill, implementing a common law for all communities in the state.

promises.notes: Uttarakhand became the first state in independent India to pass a UCC bill in early 2024. Other BJP-ruled states (such as Assam and Rajasthan) are drafting UCC bills, but no national law has been passed.
promises.supporting_quote: Uttarakhand passed the Uniform Civil Code Bill, making it the first state to implement the code. The law covers marriage, divorce, inheritance, and live-in relationships.
promises.importance_reason: minor: category 'politics' not in high-impact list

promises.promise: One nation one election
promises.source_description: PIB: Cabinet accepts Kovind Committee recommendations on One Nation One Election
promises.gemma_reasoning: Bill introduced, under examination by a Joint Parliamentary Committee.

evidence_articles.title: Union Cabinet approves 'One Nation, One Election' proposal recommended by Kovind panel
evidence_articles.rephrased: The Union Cabinet approved the simultaneous elections proposal. The implementation requires constitutional amendments and consensus among state assemblies.
evidence_articles.content: The Union Cabinet approved the high-level committee's report on One Nation One Election, paving the way for simultaneous Lok Sabha and Assembly polls.

promises.notes: The Ram Nath Kovind committee submitted its report in March 2024 recommending simultaneous polls. The Union Cabinet approved the report in September 2024, but it awaits parliamentary approval.
promises.supporting_quote: The Union Cabinet approved the simultaneous elections proposal. The implementation requires constitutional amendments and consensus among state assemblies.
promises.importance_reason: minor: category 'politics' not in high-impact list

promises.promise: Demonetization will end black money and terrorism funding
promises.source_description: PIB: Prime Minister's address to the nation on Demonetization
promises.gemma_reasoning: Demonetization failed to end black money and terrorism funding.

evidence_articles.title: Demonetisation: 99.3% of demonetised notes returned to system, says RBI
evidence_articles.rephrased: The RBI's annual report revealed that Rs 15.31 lakh crore out of Rs 15.41 lakh crore in demonetized currency notes were returned, indicating negligible black money extinguishment.
evidence_articles.content: According to the RBI Annual Report for 2017-18, about 99.3 per cent of the demonetised Rs 500 and Rs 1000 notes returned to the banking system.

promises.notes: Demonetization did not extinguish black money, as 99.3% of the banned Rs 500 and Rs 1000 notes were returned to the banking system, according to the RBI's final report.
promises.supporting_quote: The RBI's annual report revealed that Rs 15.31 lakh crore out of Rs 15.41 lakh crore in demonetized currency notes were returned, indicating negligible black money extinguishment.
promises.importance_reason: high-impact category (economy) + senior role (Prime Minister)

promises.promise: Bullet train between Mumbai and Ahmedabad by 2023
promises.source_description: PIB: Foundation stone laid for Mumbai-Ahmedabad High Speed Rail
promises.gemma_reasoning: Target deadline of 2023 passed, project delayed to 2027-2028.

evidence_articles.title: COVID snag in bullet-train project, may fail to meet 2023 deadline
evidence_articles.rephrased: The National High-Speed Rail Corporation admitted that land acquisition delays and the COVID pandemic had pushed the project completion date far past the original 2023 target.
evidence_articles.content: NHSRCL officials confirmed that the original target of December 2023 to commission the entire Mumbai-Ahmedabad bullet train project is no longer feasible.

promises.notes: The bullet train missed its original 2023 deadline due to land acquisition challenges in Maharashtra and pandemic delays. The first phase (Surat-Bilimora) is now rescheduled for 2026.
promises.supporting_quote: The National High-Speed Rail Corporation admitted that land acquisition delays and the COVID pandemic had pushed the project completion date far past the original 2023 target.
promises.importance_reason: high-impact category (infrastructure) + senior role (Prime Minister)

promises.promise: Remove Rohingyas from India completely
promises.source_description: PIB: Home Minister's remarks on border security and illegal migration
promises.gemma_reasoning: The article states ongoing action to identify and deport Rohingya immigrants.

evidence_articles.title: Shah says action underway to identify and deport illegal migrants
evidence_articles.rephrased: The Home Ministry stated that illegal migrants are identified and deported in accordance with legal processes, but deportations remain slow and subject to legal challenges.
evidence_articles.content: Union Home Minister Amit Shah reiterated that the government is taking strict action to identify and deport illegal Rohingya migrants residing in the country.

promises.notes: Deportations of Rohingya refugees occur sporadically, but legal cases and international non-refoulement conventions have prevented complete deportation, with the majority remaining in camps.
promises.supporting_quote: The Home Ministry stated that illegal migrants are identified and deported in accordance with legal processes, but deportations remain slow and subject to legal challenges.
promises.importance_reason: minor: category 'politics' not in high-impact list

promises.promise: Free and world class government schools in Delhi
promises.source_description: AAP Delhi Election Manifesto 2015
promises.gemma_reasoning: Delhi government school results improved significantly, fulfilling the promise.

evidence_articles.title: Atishi turns spotlight on 'world-class' govt school reforms
evidence_articles.rephrased: Delhi's education department highlighted infrastructure upgrades, international teacher training programs, and CBSE board pass rates as evidence of successful government school reforms.
evidence_articles.content: Delhi Education Minister Atishi highlighted that Delhi government schools have outperformed private schools in board results for seven consecutive years due to systemic educational reforms.

promises.notes: Under the AAP government, Delhi schools underwent significant reforms: improved infrastructure, teacher training, happiness curriculum introduction, and pass rates exceeding private schools.
promises.supporting_quote: Delhi's education department highlighted infrastructure upgrades, international teacher training programs, and CBSE board pass rates as evidence of successful government school reforms.
promises.importance_reason: high-impact category (education) + senior role (Chief Minister)

promises.promise: Free electricity up to 200 units in Delhi
promises.source_description: AAP Delhi Election Manifesto 2015 - Power section
promises.gemma_reasoning: Free electricity up to 200 units has been implemented and sustained in Delhi.

evidence_articles.title: Kejriwal announces free electricity up to 200 units for Delhiites
evidence_articles.rephrased: The Delhi government approved a full subsidy for households consuming up to 200 units of electricity per month, fulfilling a major manifesto pledge.
evidence_articles.content: Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal announced that consumers consuming up to 200 units of electricity per month in Delhi will not have to pay any power bill.

promises.notes: The AAP government implemented the free electricity scheme (up to 200 units fully subsidized) in 2019, which continues to benefit millions of households in Delhi.
promises.supporting_quote: The Delhi government approved a full subsidy for households consuming up to 200 units of electricity per month, fulfilling a major manifesto pledge.
promises.importance_reason: high-impact category (infrastructure) + senior role (Chief Minister)

promises.promise: Clean Yamuna river by 2025
promises.source_description: AAP Delhi Election Manifesto 2020 - Yamuna Cleanup
promises.gemma_reasoning: The target deadline of 2025 has passed.

evidence_articles.title: Delhi govt working on war footing to clean Yamuna by 2025
evidence_articles.rephrased: While the government announced action plans to clean the Yamuna by 2025, pollution monitoring reports and NGT audits show critical sewage treatment gaps remain, making the target unmet.
evidence_articles.content: Deputy CM Manish Sisodia stated the government is implementing a six-point action plan to clean the Yamuna by 2025, but experts raise concerns over the slow pace of sewage treatment plant upgrades.

promises.notes: Despite multiple action plans and a target to clean the river by 2025, NGT reports indicate the Yamuna in Delhi remains highly polluted with untreated industrial waste and sewage.
promises.supporting_quote: While the government announced action plans to clean the Yamuna by 2025, pollution monitoring reports and NGT audits show critical sewage treatment gaps remain, making the target unmet.
promises.importance_reason: high-impact category (infrastructure) + senior role (Chief Minister)

promises.promise: Guarantee minimum income of Rs 72,000 per year to poorest families (NYAY scheme)
promises.source_description: Indian National Congress Manifesto 2019 (NYAY)
promises.gemma_reasoning: Congress lost the 2019 election, so the NYAY scheme was not implemented.

evidence_articles.title: Congress promises Rs 72,000 per annum to poorest people under NYAY scheme
evidence_articles.rephrased: Congress President Rahul Gandhi announced the Nyuntam Aay Yojana (NYAY), promising to transfer Rs 72,000 annually to the poorest 20% of Indian families if voted to power.
evidence_articles.content: Congress President Rahul Gandhi on Monday announced a 'historic' minimum income guarantee scheme of Rs 72,000 per annum for five crore poorest families in the country.

promises.notes: NYAY was a minimum income guarantee promise made in the Congress 2019 manifesto. Since the party did not win the general election, the promise is marked void.
promises.supporting_quote: Congress President Rahul Gandhi announced the Nyuntam Aay Yojana (NYAY), promising to transfer Rs 72,000 annually to the poorest 20% of Indian families if voted to power.
promises.importance_reason: minor: role 'Leader of Opposition' is not senior cabinet/exec level

promises.promise: End mafia raj and crime completely in Uttar Pradesh
promises.source_description: Times of India: BJP UP Manifesto 2017 - Key highlights of Lok Kalyan Sankalp Patra
promises.gemma_reasoning: Crime stats show mixed results and encounter killings rose significantly.

evidence_articles.title: With zero tolerance for crime and criminals, U.P. on path of development: Yogi Adityanath
evidence_articles.rephrased: Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath claimed a dramatic improvement in law and order under the zero-tolerance policy, though independent observers note high numbers of extrajudicial encounters.
evidence_articles.content: Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath stated that organized crime had been completely curbed in Uttar Pradesh due to strict zero-tolerance enforcement policies.

promises.notes: This is a broad vision slogan. While Uttar Pradesh launched aggressive crackdowns on organized crime gangs and mafia networks, debate continues regarding due process and human rights compliance.
promises.supporting_quote: Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath claimed a dramatic improvement in law and order under the zero-tolerance policy, though independent observers note high numbers of extrajudicial encounters.
promises.importance_reason: minor: category 'crime_violence' not in high-impact list

promises.promise: Ma Mati Manush — government for people of Bengal
promises.source_description: Times of India: Trinamool Congress Manifesto Highlights 2011
promises.gemma_reasoning: The provided information does not indicate the promise's fulfillment or failure.

evidence_articles.title: Names of minority community people would not be omitted from voters list: Mamata
evidence_articles.rephrased: Mamata Banerjee reiterated her administration's commitment to protecting citizen identities and welfare benefits under the regional Ma Mati Manush governance framework.
evidence_articles.content: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee asserted that her government's Ma Mati Manush philosophy stands as a shield for the rights of all citizens in the state.

promises.notes: Ma Mati Manush is the foundational governing slogan and ideological vision of Mamata Banerjee's TMC administration, representing welfare and agrarian development programs in Bengal.
promises.supporting_quote: Mamata Banerjee reiterated her administration's commitment to protecting citizen identities and welfare benefits under the regional Ma Mati Manush governance framework.
promises.importance_reason: minor: category 'politics' not in high-impact list

promises.promise: Viksit Bharat by 2047 — developed India by centenary of independence
promises.source_description: PIB: PM's address to the nation on 76th Independence Day (Viksit Bharat)
promises.gemma_reasoning: Long term vision, too early to assess.

evidence_articles.title: PM Modi asks state governments to align with Viksit Bharat @ 2047 target
evidence_articles.rephrased: Prime Minister Narendra Modi urged state CMs to align local infrastructure plans with the national Viksit Bharat developed-nation targets for 2047.
evidence_articles.content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi stated that every state must coordinate with the Centre to ensure the collective realization of the developed India vision by 2047.

promises.notes: PM Modi announced the 'Viksit Bharat' vision, aiming to make India a fully developed country by 2047. NITI Aayog is currently drafting the master vision document for sector-wise targets.
promises.supporting_quote: Prime Minister Narendra Modi urged state CMs to align local infrastructure plans with the national Viksit Bharat developed-nation targets for 2047.
promises.importance_reason: high-impact category (economy) + senior role (Prime Minister)

promises.promise: Construct a model colony in Velugumatla for poor people with amenities like electricity, water, roads, and a hospital
promises.source_description: House site pattas distributed, Indiramma houses sanctioned to eligible poor people on Bhoodan lands in Velugumatla - The Hindu
promises.gemma_reasoning: The colony is planned to be completed by December 9, 2026.

evidence_articles.title: House site pattas distributed, Indiramma houses sanctioned to eligible poor people on Bhoodan lands in Velugumatla - The Hindu
evidence_articles.rephrased: **Deputy Chief Minister Mallu Bhatti Vikramarka**, during a visit to Khammam, announced plans for the construction of a model colony in Velugumatla for poor people.  The colony will include amenities like electricity, water, roads, and a hospital, with completion expected by December 9, 2026. **Mr. ...
evidence_articles.content: A model colony will be constructed with all amenities for the poor people in Velugumatla in Khammam city in such a way that people from other States in the country will come and visit it, said Deputy Chief Minister Mallu Bhatti Vikramarka. He said that the remaining 16 acres of land in Velugumatla, after distributing house sites to the poor, will be utilised for the construction of a Telangana Pub...

promises.notes: The colony is planned to be completed by December 9, 2026.
promises.supporting_quote: **Deputy Chief Minister Mallu Bhatti Vikramarka**, during a visit to Khammam, announced plans for the construction of a model colony in Velugumatla for poor people.  The colony will include amenities like electricity, water, roads, and a hospital, with completion expected by December 9, 2026. **Mr. ...
promises.importance_reason: minor: category 'general' not in high-impact list, role 'Politician' is not senior cabinet/exec level

promises.promise: Expedite construction of newly-sanctioned medical colleges
promises.source_description: Minister urged to expedite construction work on medical colleges - The Hindu
promises.gemma_reasoning: The article states the colleges are nearing completion and require minimal funding and administrative action.

evidence_articles.title: Minister urged to expedite construction work on medical colleges - The Hindu
evidence_articles.rephrased: **Praja Arogya Vedika (PAV)**, an organization advocating for medical education, urged **Minister for Health, Medical Education and Family Welfare, Satya Kumar Yadav**, to expedite the remaining construction work on newly-sanctioned medical colleges. PAV emphasized that these colleges are nearing co...
evidence_articles.content: Praja Arogya Vedika (PAV) wrote to Minister for Health, Medical Education and Family Welfare, Satya Kumar Yadav, urging him to expedite the remaining construction work on newly-sanctioned medical colleges and operationalise them at the earliest, before the beginning of the academic year, for the benefit of students. In the letter, they wrote that the construction of medical colleges and associated...

promises.notes: The article states the colleges are nearing completion and require minimal funding and administrative action.
promises.supporting_quote: **Praja Arogya Vedika (PAV)**, an organization advocating for medical education, urged **Minister for Health, Medical Education and Family Welfare, Satya Kumar Yadav**, to expedite the remaining construction work on newly-sanctioned medical colleges. PAV emphasized that these colleges are nearing co...
promises.importance_reason: minor: category 'general' not in high-impact list, role 'Politician' is not senior cabinet/exec level

promises.promise: Recruit 400 permanent veterinary officers
promises.source_description: Recruitment of 400 veterinary officers under way through KPSC: Minister - The Hindu
promises.gemma_reasoning: The recruitment process is underway, but no completion date is mentioned.

evidence_articles.title: Recruitment of 400 veterinary officers under way through KPSC: Minister - The Hindu
evidence_articles.rephrased: **K. Venkatesh**, the **Animal Husbandry Minister** of Karnataka, announced that the recruitment process for 400 vacant permanent veterinary officer positions is underway through the **Karnataka Public Service Commission**.  The appointments are expected to address the shortage of veterinary doctors...
evidence_articles.content: Animal Husbandry Minister K. Venkatesh on Wednesday said the process to fill 400 vacant permanent posts of veterinary officers in the State is under way through the Karnataka Public Service Commission (KPSC) and the recruitment is expected to be completed shortly. Replying to BJP members D.S. Arun, Hemalatha Nayak, and S.V. Sankanur in the Legislative Council, the Minister said the appointments wo...

promises.notes: The recruitment process is underway, but no completion date is mentioned.
promises.supporting_quote: **K. Venkatesh**, the **Animal Husbandry Minister** of Karnataka, announced that the recruitment process for 400 vacant permanent veterinary officer positions is underway through the **Karnataka Public Service Commission**.  The appointments are expected to address the shortage of veterinary doctors...
promises.importance_reason: minor: category 'general' not in high-impact list, role 'Politician' is not senior cabinet/exec level

promises.promise: Pay a 'Ugadi Bonus' of ₹1 per litre to its 1.27 lakh milk farmers
promises.source_description: Ugadi bonus from BAMUL to milk producers - The Hindu
promises.gemma_reasoning: The article states this is a decision made and will be paid out, but doesn't confirm if it's a one-time or recurring bonus.

evidence_articles.title: Ugadi bonus from BAMUL to milk producers - The Hindu
evidence_articles.rephrased: **Bengaluru Milk Union Ltd. (BAMUL)** is set to report a record turnover of ₹3,400 crore by the end of the 2025-2026 financial year and has decided to pay a 'Ugadi Bonus' of ₹1 per litre to its 1.27 lakh milk farmers. The bonus will cost approximately ₹59.39 crore and comes after BAMUL reported a pr...
evidence_articles.content: As Bengaluru Milk Union Ltd. (BAMUL) is expected to report a record turnover of about ₹3,400 crore by the end of the 2025-2026 financial year, it has decided to pay ₹1 per litre as ‘Ugadi Bonus’ to 1.27 lakh farmers from whom milk is procured from April 1 to March 31, 2026. This year, BAMUL has procured 59.39 crore litres of milk this year, and the ‘Ugadi Bonus’ will entail an expenditure of about...

promises.notes: The article states this is a decision made and will be paid out, but doesn't confirm if it's a one-time or recurring bonus.
promises.supporting_quote: **Bengaluru Milk Union Ltd. (BAMUL)** is set to report a record turnover of ₹3,400 crore by the end of the 2025-2026 financial year and has decided to pay a 'Ugadi Bonus' of ₹1 per litre to its 1.27 lakh milk farmers. The bonus will cost approximately ₹59.39 crore and comes after BAMUL reported a pr...
promises.importance_reason: minor: category 'general' not in high-impact list, role 'MP' is not senior cabinet/exec level

promises.promise: Ban illegal tree felling in forest areas
promises.source_description: Tree felling banned in view of likely increase in demand for firewood - The Hindu
promises.gemma_reasoning: The article states the ban is in effect and officials are instructed to enforce it.

evidence_articles.title: Tree felling banned in view of likely increase in demand for firewood - The Hindu
evidence_articles.rephrased: **Minister for Forests and Ecology Eshwar Khandre** has banned illegal tree felling in various forest areas, including government plantations and roadside plantations. This follows concerns raised by **wildlife conservationist Giridhar Kulkarni**, who warned of increased demand for firewood due to L...
evidence_articles.content: Minister for Forests and Ecology Eshwar Khandre has banned illegal felling of trees in forests, forest edges, government plantations, government land and roadside plantations. This follows the likelihood of an increased demand for firewood in the wake of the U.S.-Israel and Iran war. He has asked officials to carry out inspection and launch vigilance activities to prevent tree felling. In a note r...

promises.notes: The article states the ban is in effect and officials are instructed to enforce it.
promises.supporting_quote: **Minister for Forests and Ecology Eshwar Khandre** has banned illegal tree felling in various forest areas, including government plantations and roadside plantations. This follows concerns raised by **wildlife conservationist Giridhar Kulkarni**, who warned of increased demand for firewood due to L...
promises.importance_reason: minor: category 'general' not in high-impact list, role 'Politician' is not senior cabinet/exec level

promises.promise: Complete eradication of Maoism
promises.source_description: 108 Maoists surrender in Chhattisgarh - The Hindu
promises.gemma_reasoning: The article states Shah set a deadline of March 31, 2026, for the eradication of Maoism.

evidence_articles.title: 108 Maoists surrender in Chhattisgarh - The Hindu
evidence_articles.rephrased: **Ten** **Maoist** rebels surrendered to Indian security forces in Chhattisgarh, bringing the total number of surrendered fighters to 108.
